name: Pea Soup with Pork
recipeIngredient: 1 lb (2 cups) dried yellow Swedish peas - domestic
1 medium Onion, whole, peeled and studded with 2 cloves
1 lb Lean salt pork, in one piece
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the dried peas in cold running water.
2. Place them in a 2-3 quart saucepan.
3. Cover with 5 cups cold water and bring to a boil over high heat.
4. Boil briskly for 2 or 3 minutes, then turn off the heat and let the peas soak in the water for an hour.
5. Skim off any pea husks that may have risen to the surface.
6. Add the finely chopped onion, whole onion, salt pork, marjoram, and thyme.
7. Bring to a boil again.
8. Simmer with the pot partially covered for about 1 1/4 hours or until peas are quite tender but have not fallen apart.
9. Remove the whole onion and salt pork from the soup.
10. Cut the pork into slices about 1/4 inch thick.
11. Serve in either of two variations:
a. Place a few slices of pork in individual serving bowls.
b. Season the hot soup with salt to taste and ladle it over the pork.
12. Alternatively, serve the soup alone, accompanied by a separate place for the sliced salt pork and spicy brown mustard.
NOTE: If domestic yellow split peas are used, they need not be soaked for an hour. Wash them carefully, cover with 4 cups cold water, and proceed with the recipe as above. They will take somewhat less time to cook than the Swedish peas.
